WARNING: This data is ONLY for CSE and CS branch.
course_objectives: To provide insight about computer network concepts.
To gain comprehensive knowledge of layered communication architectures, functionalities, key protocols as well as security issues related with each layer.
course_outcomes: CO1: Apply the knowledge of networking concepts and functionality of physical layer.
CO2: Explore the concept of elementary data link layer protocol to build a robust network.
CO3: Analyze the concept of routing and IP addressing in network layer.
CO4: Examine the usage and working of transport layer.
CO5: Determine the performance of different protocols used at application layer.

units.title: Introduction - Physical Layer
topics: Introduction to computer networks: Network applications, Mode of communications
Reference models: OSI, TCP/IP layers, and characteristics
Physical Layer: Network devices, Network topology

units.title: Data Link Layer
topics: Data Link layer: Design issues, Framing
Flow control: Elementary Data Link Protocols, Sliding Window protocols
Multiple access protocols: Random Access (ALOHA, CSMA, CSMA/CD, CSMA/CA)
Controlled Access: Polling, Reservation, Token Passing
IEEE standards Wired LANs (Ethernet): Traditional, Fast, Gigabit Ethernet

units.title: Network Layer
topics: Logical addressing: Basic internetworking, Subnetting, Supernetting, NAT
Routing algorithms and protocols: Shortest Path Algorithms, Flooding
Distance Vector Routing, Link State Routing
Hierarchical Routing, Broadcast Routing, Multicast Routing, Anycast Routing

units.title: Transport Layer
topics: Transport layer protocols: UDP and TCP, SCTP
Congestion control in TCP: Open Loop, Closed Loop choke packets
Quality of service (QoS): Leaky bucket algorithm, Token bucket algorithm

units.title: Application Layer
topics: Session Layer: Roles and responsibility of session layer protocols
Symmetric-key cryptography, Asymmetric-key cryptography, Digital Signature

textbooks: Behrouz Forouzan - Data Communication and Networking, Fourth Edition-2006, Tata McGraw Hill
Andrew Tanenbaum - Computer Networks, Fifth Edition-2011, Prentice Hall
William Stallings - Data and Computer Communication, Eighth Edition-2008, Pearson
reference_books: Kurose and Ross - Computer Networking: A Top-Down Approach, Eighth Edition-2021, Pearson
Peterson and Davie - Computer Networks: A Systems Approach, Fourth Edition-1996, Morgan Kaufmann
Behrouz Forouzan - TCP/IP Protocol Suite, McGraw Hill
